_comment: Worked example for the doc-consistency skill, modelled on the agent-governance-framework (AGF) public repo. Place a real manifest as '.doc-consistency.json' at the repo root and run: python .claude/skills/doc-consistency/check_doc_consistency.py /path/to/.doc-consistency.json . 'root' is resolved relative to the manifest's own directory; set it to the repo root. Each check pins a cross-referenced value to an authoritative source ('expect') and lists every doc that asserts it ('asserted_in'). Each asserted_in 'pattern' is a Python regex with exactly ONE capture group = the value the doc claims. JSON has no comments, so this _comment key is simply ignored by the checker (it only reads 'root' and 'checks').
